detail: evidence points at expected_dead_components but list is empty

original_draft.source_quote: Board completely unresponsive after being left plugged-in while off — no LCD, no LPC wakeup indication, nothing happens when the power key is pressed. Try the LPC reset button (nearest the battery connector); if that fails, the only reliable recovery is disconnecting and reconnecting the battery cables.

evidence.reasoning: U14 is the source_refdes of LPC_VCC in the graph, identifying it as the LPC controller MCU referenced by 'LPC wakeup indication'.
Complete unresponsiveness with no LCD and no wakeup is modeled as the LPC being dead from the system's perspective.
With LPC (U14) dead, LPC_VCC (sourced by U14) is effectively offline.

evidence.source_quote_substring: nothing happens when the power key is pressed
evidence.reasoning: Without LPC driving SOM power enable, the CPU SOM never boots.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: LPC controller (U14, source of LPC_VCC) locks up in an unrecoverable firmware state; board appears completely dead with no LCD or wake activity. Modeled as LPC dead, which blocks SOM power enable.

original_draft.source_quote: When the charge board is dead, other community posts describe connecting pins 2 and 3 on the J1 plug to bypass it temporarily and allow firmware reflashing while a replacement board is sourced.

evidence.source_quote_substring: pins 2 and 3 on the J1 plug
evidence.reasoning: J1 is the battery/charge-board connector on the motherboard, per registry and graph.
A dead charge board presents as the J1 interface not delivering battery power — modeled as an open.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: Charge board failure breaks the battery path at J1; cell rails BAT1..BAT8 and aggregated BAT are unavailable to the motherboard.

original_draft.source_quote: It stopped working: it was not turning on, and when the power supply was connected (both USB-C PD 20 V and the enclosed 24 V adapter) the D9 LED was dark. Verify cell voltages individually; reflash LPC firmware via a second computer.

evidence.reasoning: Recovery path is to reflash LPC firmware, implicating U14 (LPC controller, source of LPC_VCC).
LPC corrupt/unresponsive such that nothing boots is modeled as LPC dead.
With LPC (U14) dead, LPC_VCC offline and downstream sequencing never runs.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: After deep-discharge event, LPC firmware on U14 is corrupted and board will not turn on; D9 stays dark despite valid adapters. Modeled as LPC dead.
